name: nl2code-v1
description: Owned NLâ†’code benchmark for semantex v0.3 â€” 50 hand-curated NL queries against permissively-licensed OSS repos, designed to exercise the Q21-class vocabulary gap (zero token overlap between query and matching code).

schema.question: NL question â€” must use everyday English, not identifiers from the target code
schema.target_repo_url: git URL of the answer-bearing repo
schema.target_sha: pinned commit SHA â€” fixed forever for reproducibility
schema.expected_files: repo-relative paths where the answer lives (file-level ground truth)
schema.expected_chunks: optional: line ranges within expected_files for stricter chunk-level grading
schema.min_recall_target: float in [0,1] â€” what file-level recall constitutes a passing answer (default 0.5)
schema.curator: github_login of the curator (humans only; LLM-drafted rows must be human-reviewed before status flips to active)
schema.notes: free-form curation notes â€” why this query, what makes it hard

totals.blind_set_curator: external contributor â€” per spec Â§4.4 anti-gaming clause
totals._note: All 10 seeded rows are 'needs_curation' until a human verifies the SHA and expected_files. b2_nl2code.py runs only rows with status='active'.

queries.question: graceful shutdown handling when the process receives an interrupt signal
queries.notes: Seeded as an example shape â€” needs SHA + reviewer-verified expected_files.

queries.question: limiting the number of concurrent operations to avoid resource exhaustion
validating that a value falls within an expected set of choices
queries.notes: Concept: enum validation. Bridges 'set of choices' â†’ ChoiceValidator.

queries.question: executing user-supplied code in a sandboxed environment
computing a stable hash so two equivalent objects produce the same key
deciding whether a node is inside a function that returns nothing
queries.notes: Concept: void-return-type analysis. NLâ†’identifier bridge required.

queries.question: writing a value to multiple destinations in parallel and waiting for all to finish
deferring cleanup that must run even if the function panics
queries.notes: Concept: defer + recover semantics. Hard for token-overlap retrieval.

queries.question: publishing an event to multiple subscribers without coupling the publisher to subscriber classes
starting a transaction that automatically rolls back when an exception is thrown
